seoTitle: How to Add Drill Through Detail Pages in Power BI
metaDescription: Learn how to set up drill-through in Power BI, letting users click from summary visuals into detailed pages for deeper, more interactive analysis.
intro: Summary numbers are useful, but sometimes viewers need to click through to see exactly what's behind a figure without leaving the report. This guide covers how to set up drill-through in Power BI, turning summary visuals into gateways for detailed pages. It's a feature that makes reports feel far more interactive, especially in industries where granular analysis matters, like finance or healthcare.

steps.heading: Set up your detail page
steps.body: Create a new report page that will act as the destination for drill-through, and add the visuals you want to show when someone drills into a specific data point.

steps.heading: Configure drill-through options
steps.body: In the detail page's settings, add the field that acts as the drill-through trigger, such as a customer name or product category, under the Drill Through section.

steps.heading: Connect it to your summary visuals
steps.body: Right-click a data point on your summary page and confirm the drill-through option now appears, navigating to the detail page filtered to that specific selection.

steps.heading: Apply best practices for implementation
steps.body: Add a clear back button on the detail page and keep the drill-through field consistent so users always land on a page filtered exactly as expected.

faq.question: Can I drill through on more than one field at a time?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure multiple drill-through fields on a detail page, letting users navigate to it from different summary categories.

faq.question: Does drill-through work the same way in the Power BI mobile app?
faq.answer: Drill-through is generally supported on mobile, though the interaction method, like a long-press instead of a right-click, can differ from the desktop experience.

faq.question: Can I hide the detail page from the main navigation?
faq.answer: Yes, detail pages are commonly hidden from the page tabs so they're only reachable through the drill-through action rather than direct navigation.

faq.question: Is there a difference between drill-through and drill-down in Power BI?
faq.answer: Drill-down expands a single visual's hierarchy in place, while drill-through navigates to an entirely separate, filtered page for deeper detail.
